1	import json
2	import os
3	import uuid
4	from typing import List, Optional
5	
6	from .models import Bookmark
7	
8	DEFAULT_DB_PATH = os.path.expanduser("~/.bookmarks.json")
9	
10	
11	def load_bookmarks(db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> List[Bookmark]:
12	    """Load all bookmarks from the JSON file."""
13	    if not os.path.exists(db_path):
14	        return []
15	    with open(db_path, "r") as f:
16	        data = json.load(f)
17	    return [Bookmark.from_dict(item) for item in data]
18	
19	
20	def save_bookmarks(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None:
21	    """Save all bookmarks to the JSON file."""
22	    data = [b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks]
23	    with open(db_path, "w") as f:
24	        json.dump(data, f, indent=2)
25	
26	
27	def add_bookmark(
28	    url: str,
29	    title: str,
30	    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
31	    notes: str = "",
32	    db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH,
33	) -> Bookmark:
34	    """Create and persist a new bookmark. Returns the created bookmark."""
35	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
36	    bookmark = Bookmark(
37	        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
38	        url=url,
39	        title=title,
40	        tags=tags or [],
41	        notes=notes,
42	    )
43	    bookmarks.append(bookmark)
44	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
45	    return bookmark
46	
47	
48	def delete_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> bool:
49	    """Delete a bookmark by its id. Returns True if found and deleted."""
50	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
51	    original_len = len(bookmarks)
52	    bookmarks = [b for b in bookmarks if b.id != bookmark_id]
53	    if len(bookmarks) == original_len:
54	        return False
55	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
56	    return True
57	
58	
59	def get_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> Optional[Bookmark]:
60	    """Retrieve a single bookmark by its id."""
61	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
62	    for b in bookmarks:
63	        if b.id == bookmark_id:
64	            return b
65	    return None

8	def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
9	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
10	        prog="bookmark",
11	        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
12	    )
13	    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")
14	
15	    # --- add ---
16	    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
17	    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
18	    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
19	    add_parser.add_argument(
20	        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
21	    )
22	    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")
23	
24	    # --- list ---
25	    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
26	    list_parser.add_argument(
27	        "--format",
28	        choices=["table", "json"],
29	        default="table",
30	        help="Output format (default: table)",
31	    )
32	
33	    # --- delete ---
34	    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
35	    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")
36	
37	    # --- show ---
38	    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
39	    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")
40	
41	    return parser
42	
43	
44	def main(argv=None):
45	    parser = build_parser()
46	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
47	
48	    if args.command is None:
49	        parser.print_help()
50	        sys.exit(1)
51	
52	    if args.command == "add":
53	        bookmark = add_bookmark(
54	            url=args.url,
55	            title=args.title,
56	            tags=args.tags,
57	            notes=args.notes,
58	        )
59	        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")
60	
61	    elif args.command == "list":
62	        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
63	        if args.format == "json":
64	            print(format_json(bookmarks))

67	
68	    elif args.command == "delete":

2	from typing import List
3	
4	from .models import Bookmark
5	
6	
7	def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
8	    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
9	    if not bookmarks:
10	        return "No bookmarks found."
11	
12	    # Column widths
13	    id_w = 8
14	    title_w = 30
15	    url_w = 40
16	    tags_w = 20
17	
18	    header = (
19	        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
20	        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
21	        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
22	        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
23	    )
24	    separator = "-" * len(header)
25	    lines = [header, separator]
26	
27	    for b in bookmarks:
28	        short_id = b.id[:8]
29	        title = b.title[:title_w]
30	        url = b.url[:url_w]
31	        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
32	        lines.append(
33	            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
34	            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
35	            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
36	            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
37	        )
38	
39	    return "\n".join(lines)
40	
41	
42	def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
43	    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
44	    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

6	
7	def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
8	    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
9	    if not bookmarks:
10	        return "No bookmarks found."
11	
12	    # Column widths
13	    id_w = 8
14	    title_w = 30
15	    url_w = 40
16	    tags_w = 20
17	
18	    header = (
19	        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
20	        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
21	        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
22	        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
23	    )
24	    separator = "-" * len(header)
25	    lines = [header, separator]
26	
27	    for b in bookmarks:
28	        short_id = b.id[:8]
29	        title = b.title[:title_w]
30	        url = b.url[:url_w]
31	        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
32	        lines.append(
33	            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
34	            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
35	            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
36	            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
37	        )
38	
39	    return "\n".join(lines)
40	
41	
42	def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
43	    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
44	    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

5	from .formatter import format_table, format_json
6	
7	
8	def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
9	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
10	        prog="bookmark",
11	        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
12	    )
13	    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")
14	
15	    # --- add ---
16	    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
17	    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
18	    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
19	    add_parser.add_argument(
20	        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
21	    )
22	    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")
23	
24	    # --- list ---
25	    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
26	    list_parser.add_argument(
27	        "--format",
28	        choices=["table", "json"],
29	        default="table",
30	        help="Output format (default: table)",
31	    )
32	
33	    # --- delete ---
34	    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
35	    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")
36	
37	    # --- show ---
38	    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
39	    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")
40	
41	    return parser
42	
43	
44	def main(argv=None):
45	    parser = build_parser()
46	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
47	
48	    if args.command is None:
49	        parser.print_help()
50	        sys.exit(1)
51	
52	    if args.command == "add":
53	        bookmark = add_bookmark(
54	            url=args.url,
55	            title=args.title,
56	            tags=args.tags,
57	            notes=args.notes,
58	        )
59	        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")
60	
61	    elif args.command == "list":
62	        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
63	        if args.format == "json":
64	            print(format_json(bookmarks))
65	        else:
66	            print(format_table(bookmarks))
67	
68	    elif args.command == "delete":
69	        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
70	        if success:
71	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
72	        else:
73	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
74	            sys.exit(1)
75	
76	    elif args.command == "show":
77	        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
78	        if bookmark is None:
79	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
80	            sys.exit(1)
81	        print(format_table([bookmark]))
82	
83	
84	if __name__ == "__main__":
85	    main()

**Core Functions:**
1. **load_bookmarks(db_path)** - Loads all bookmarks from JSON file (~/.bookmarks.json by default), returns empty list if file doesn't exist
2. **save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)** - Writes bookmarks to JSON file with pretty-printing (indent=2)
3. **add_bookmark(url, title, tags=None, notes="", db_path)** - Creates new bookmark with UUID, appends to file, returns Bookmark object
4. **delete_bookmark(bookmark_id, db_path)** - Removes bookmark by ID, returns True if found/deleted, False otherwise
5. **get_bookmark(bookmark_id, db_path)** - Retrieves single bookmark by ID, returns None if not found

**Default database location:** `~/.bookmarks.json` (user's home directory)

### 5. CLI COMMANDS & STRUCTURE

Location: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775488091772-uyyy1c/src/cli.py`

**Architecture:** Uses `argparse` with subcommands pattern

**Existing Commands:**

1. **add** - Add a new bookmark
   - Positional args: `url`, `title`
   - Options: `--tags` (nargs="*", optional tags), `--notes` (optional notes)
   - Output: "Bookmark added: {id}"

2. **list** - List all bookmarks
   - Options: `--format` (choices: "table" or "json", default "table")
   - Output: Formatted bookmarks or JSON array

3. **delete** - Delete a bookmark by ID
   - Positional arg: `id`
   - Output: "Bookmark {id} deleted." or error message with sys.exit(1)

4. **show** - Show a single bookmark
   - Positional arg: `id`
   - Output: Formatted as table or error message with sys.exit(1)

**Entry Point:** `main(argv=None)` function called by CLI entry point

### 6. OUTPUT FORMATTING

Location: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775488091772-uyyy1c/src/formatter.py`

**format_table(bookmarks)** - Plain-text table format:
- Column layout: ID (8 chars) | Title (30 chars) | URL (40 chars) | Tags (20 chars)
- Shows first 8 chars of UUID as ID
- Truncates long values to column width
- Returns "No bookmarks found." if empty
- Creates header and separator line

**format_json(bookmarks)** - JSON format:
- Uses `json.dumps()` with `indent=2` for pretty-printing
- Calls `bookmark.to_dict()` on each bookmark
- Returns valid JSON string

### 7. TEST STRUCTURE & PATTERNS

Location: `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775488091772-uyyy1c/tests/`

**test_storage.py** - Storage layer tests (117 lines)
- Uses pytest fixtures: `db_path` (temporary file), `sample_bookmarks` (2 test bookmarks)
- Test classes organized by function being tested:
  - `TestLoadBookmarks` - 2 tests (missing file, loading from file)
  - `TestSaveBookmarks` - 2 tests (file creation, JSON validity)
  - `TestAddBookmark` - 3 tests (returns bookmark, persists, unique IDs)
  - `TestDeleteBookmark` - 2 tests (deletes existing, returns false for missing)
  - `TestGetBookmark` - 2 tests (returns by ID, returns None for missing)

**test_search.py** - Search functionality tests (180 lines)
- **Important note:** These tests define EXPECTED behavior for a search feature that DOESN'T YET EXIST in the codebase
- Uses fixture `bookmarks` with 5 sample bookmarks of various types (Python, RunPod, JavaScript, Flask, argparse docs)
- Test classes for functions that need to be implemented:
  - `TestSearchByTitle` - Case-insensitive substring matching
  - `TestSearchByTag` - Exact tag matching
  - `TestSearchByDomain` - Domain extraction and matching (supports subdomains)
  - `TestSearchCombined` - Combined search with AND logic across criteria
  - `TestSearchCLI` - CLI integration tests (parser validation for "search" command)

**Testing Patterns Used:**
- Class-based test organization
- pytest fixtures for reusable test data and temporary files
- Descriptive test method names (`test_*`)
- Clear assertions with direct value comparisons
- Parametric test organization (multiple related tests in a class)

### 8. UTILITY FUNCTIONS & EXISTING FEATURES

**Search Functions (NEEDED - Not Yet Implemented):**
According to test_search.py, these functions need to be created in `src/search.py`:
- `search_by_title(bookmarks, query)` - Case-insensitive substring search in title
- `search_by_tag(bookmarks, tag)` - Exact match search in tags list
- `search_by_domain(bookmarks, domain)` - Extract domain from URL and match (including subdomains)
- `search(bookmarks, title=None, tag=None, domain=None)` - Combined search with AND logic (all specified criteria must match)

**CLI Search Integration Needed:**
- New "search" subcommand in parser
- Support for `--title`, `--tag`, `--domain` options
- Support for `--format` option (json/table)
- Integration with main() command handler

**Existing UUID Generation:**
- Uses `uuid.uuid4()` in `add_bookmark()` function
- Stored as string in bookmark.id

### 9. KEY INSIGHTS

1. **Single Responsibility:** Layers are well-separated:
   - models.py = data structure only
   - storage.py = persistence (JSON file operations)
   - formatter.py = presentation only
   - cli.py = command orchestration

2. **Stateless Functions:** All functions are pure (no global state), making them testable

3. **Default Database Location:** `~/.bookmarks.json` is standard, all functions accept optional `db_path` parameter for testing

4. **Extensibility:** The test file reveals the intended next feature (search), with well-defined expected behavior through tests-first approach

5. **Error Handling:** Commands exit with code 1 on errors (bookmark not found); success prints message to stdout

6. **No Validation Layer:** Models and storage don't validate inputs (e.g., URL format, required fields)

import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import json
from typing import List

from .models import Bookmark


def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
    if not bookmarks:
        return "No bookmarks found."

    # Column widths
    id_w = 8
    title_w = 30
    url_w = 40
    tags_w = 20

    header = (
        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
    )
    separator = "-" * len(header)
    lines = [header, separator]

    for b in bookmarks:
        short_id = b.id[:8]
        title = b.title[:title_w]
        url = b.url[:url_w]
        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
        lines.append(
            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
        )

    return "\n".join(lines)


def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

# Plan: Add `search` Command to Bookmark CLI

## Context
The bookmark CLI (`src/cli.py`) supports `add`, `list`, `delete`, and `show` commands but has no way to filter bookmarks. A `test_search.py` already exists with 180 lines of failing tests that fully specify the required behavior — making this a test-driven implementation. Two things need to be created/modified: a new `src/search.py` module and a new subcommand wired into `src/cli.py`.

---

## Critical Files

| File | Action | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| `src/search.py` | **Create** | Pure search functions |
| `src/cli.py` | **Modify** | Add `search` subparser + handler |
| `tests/test_search.py` | Read-only reference | Defines all required behavior |

---

## Step-by-Step Implementation

### Step 1 — Create `src/search.py`

New file with four functions (exactly what `test_search.py` imports):

```python
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import List, Optional
from .models import Bookmark


def search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], query: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Case-insensitive substring match on title."""
    q = query.lower()
    return [b for b in bookmarks if q in b.title.lower()]


def search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Exact match: tag must appear in the bookmark's tags list."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if tag in b.tags]


def search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Match hostname exactly OR as a parent domain (subdomain support).
    e.g. 'python.org' matches 'docs.python.org'.
    """
    def _matches(url: str) -> bool:
        hostname = urlparse(url).netloc
        return hostname == domain or hostname.endswith(f".{domain}")
    return [b for b in bookmarks if _matches(b.url)]


def search(
    bookmarks: List[Bookmark],
    title: Optional[str] = None,
    tag: Optional[str] = None,
    domain: Optional[str] = None,
) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """AND-logic search across all provided criteria.
    Criteria that are None are ignored; no criteria → returns all bookmarks.
    """
    result = bookmarks
    if title is not None:
        result = search_by_title(result, title)
    if tag is not None:
        result = search_by_tag(result, tag)
    if domain is not None:
        result = search_by_domain(result, domain)
    return result
```

**Key decisions:**
- `search_by_title`: `.lower()` on both sides → case-insensitive substring match
- `search_by_tag`: plain `in` on the list → exact match (no case folding)
- `search_by_domain`: `urlparse(url).netloc` extracts the hostname; supports both exact and parent-domain matches
- `search()`: applies filters sequentially (AND logic naturally falls out of chaining)

---

### Step 2 — Modify `src/cli.py`

**2a. Add the `search` subparser** inside `build_parser()`, after the `show` parser block (before `return parser`):

```python
# --- search ---
search_parser = subparsers.add_parser("search", help="Search bookmarks")
search_parser.add_argument(
    "--title", default=None, help="Filter by title substring (case-insensitive)"
)
search_parser.add_argument(
    "--tag", default=None, help="Filter by tag (exact match)"
)
search_parser.add_argument(
    "--domain", default=None, help="Filter by domain"
)
search_parser.add_argument(
    "--format",
    choices=["table", "json"],
    default="table",
    help="Output format (default: table)",
)
```

**2b. Add the `search` command handler** in `main()`, after the `show` block:

```python
elif args.command == "search":
    from .search import search
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
    results = search(bookmarks, title=args.title, tag=args.tag, domain=args.domain)
    if args.format == "json":
        print(format_json(results))
    else:
        print(format_table(results))
```

The import is local to keep the top of `cli.py` clean (storage/formatter imports remain unchanged).

---

## Test Coverage Mapping

| Test class | Tests | Covered by |
|---|---|---|
| `TestSearchByTitle` | case-insensitive, substring, no-match | `search_by_title` |
| `TestSearchByTag` | exact, shared tag, no-match | `search_by_tag` |
| `TestSearchByDomain` | domain, subdomain, no-match | `search_by_domain` |
| `TestSearchCombined` | single/multi criteria, AND logic, no-criteria, conflicting | `search` |
| `TestSearchCLI` | parser accepts `search` + `--title`/`--tag`/`--format` | `build_parser` |

---

## Verification

```bash
# Run all tests — both existing storage tests and new search tests must pass
pytest

# Manual smoke test
bookmark add https://python.org "Python Home" --tags python docs
bookmark search --title python
bookmark search --tag docs --format json
bookmark search --domain python.org
bookmark search --title python --tag docs --domain python.org
```

import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    # --- search ---
    search_parser = subparsers.add_parser("search", help="Search bookmarks")
    search_parser.add_argument(
        "--title", default=None, help="Filter by title substring (case-insensitive)"
    )
    search_parser.add_argument(
        "--tag", default=None, help="Filter by tag (exact match)"
    )
    search_parser.add_argument(
        "--domain", default=None, help="Filter by domain"
    )
    search_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))

    elif args.command == "search":
        from .search import search
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        results = search(bookmarks, title=args.title, tag=args.tag, domain=args.domain)
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(results))
        else:
            print(format_table(results))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
